statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 19 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel category, though overall averages remain near mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure with widespread reserve depletion across member states. While EU-wide averages for petrol (98.9 days) and diesel (97.8 days) remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum, jet fuel reserves (91.9 days) sit perilously close to the threshold. The data reveals a stark geographic divide, with Eastern European nations bearing the brunt of the supply strain.

Romania presents the most concerning situation with all three fuel categories in critical status - petrol at just 33.2 days and diesel at 32.4 days, representing less than half the required reserves. Poland, Luxembourg, and Germany also show critical levels across multiple fuel types. The widespread nature of these shortfalls is alarming: 19 of 27 EU countries currently hold critical reserves in at least one fuel category.

Oil prices continue climbing pressure, with Brent crude reaching $106.26 per barrel - up nearly 27% since Middle East supply disruptions began. This translates to significant price variations across the EU, with consumers in the Netherlands paying €2.279 per liter for petrol compared to €1.34 in Malta. The combination of depleted strategic reserves and elevated crude prices leaves the EU vulnerable to further supply shocks.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries hold critical fuel reserves in at least one category, indicating widespread depletion across the bloc
Romania faces the most severe shortfall with petrol and diesel reserves at just 33.2 and 32.4 days respectively - well below the 90-day mandate
Jet fuel reserves EU-wide average only 91.9 days, barely above the mandatory minimum and creating aviation sector vulnerability
Brent crude oil has surged 27% to $106.26/barrel since Middle East conflicts began, driving significant price disparities across member states
Major economies including Germany, France, and Italy all show critical status in multiple fuel categories, amplifying systemic risk
